title: LRU Cache
description:
Design a data structure that follows the **Least Recently Used (LRU)** cache eviction policy.

Your function receives:
- `capacity`: the max number of items
- `operations`: array of `"get"` or `"put"` strings
- `args`: array of argument arrays (`[key]` for get, `[key, value]` for put)

Return an array of results: `get` returns the value or `-1` if not found; `put` returns `null`.

Example: capacity=2, ops=["put","put","get","put","get","put","get","get","get"], args=[[1,1],[2,2],[1],[3,3],[2],[4,4],[1],[3],[4]] → [null,null,1,null,-1,null,-1,3,4]
hints: You need O(1) get and put — combine a hash map with a doubly linked list.
The list maintains access order; the map gives O(1) key lookup.
On access: move the node to the front. On eviction: remove from the tail.
